title: Multiagent Continual Coordination via Progressive Task Contextualization
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: The paper introduces a continual coordination framework in cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) where tasks appear sequentially. The authors develop a multi-agent task context extraction module using a product-of-expert (POE) mechanism, an expandable multi-head policy architecture, and policy distillation to approximate policy head selection. They propose a method called MACPro and evaluate it on benchmarks like level-based foraging (LBF), predator-prey (PP), and StarCraft Multi-Agent Challenge (SMAC).
problem_statement: Mainstream cooperative MARL methods are restricted to being trained in one single task or multiple tasks simultaneously, assuming agents have access to data from all tasks at all times, which is unrealistic for physical agents in the real world that can only attend to one task at a time.

methodology.approach: Continual coordination framework in cooperative MARL
components: Multi-agent task context extraction module using product-of-expert (POE) mechanism
Contrastive regularizer to optimize learned representation
Expandable multi-head policy architecture with independent heads
Policy distillation for approximating policy head selection process
CTDE (Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution) paradigm

contributions: Development of a continual coordination framework in cooperative MARL
Evaluation on various benchmarks in continual setting
